question: Is there a dedicated API for batch-importing user data from a JSON file? Which endpoint accepts JSON-format user imports?

No JSON-based batch import API exists in the system

annotation.notes: Unanswerable: import only supports Excel, not JSON. Distractor: importData endpoint exists but for Excel only.

question: How does the system implement user logout? Why does the frontend logout request not appear as a mapped endpoint in any controller class?

question: The frontend cache management module calls a function to retrieve cache keys for a given cache name. Can the static index trace this request to its backend endpoint? If not, why?

The backend endpoint is GET /monitor/cache/getKeys/{cacheName} in CacheController

question: Trace the call chain for the frontend POST /logout request. Which backend controller method handles this request?

forbidden_claims.claim: A controller class maps POST /logout via @PostMapping or @RequestMapping
forbidden_claims.reason: POST /logout is configured via Spring Security LogoutFilter, not any controller annotation

The static index has no DEFINES_API entity for /logout
LogoutSuccessHandlerImpl handles the logout response but is not a controller

question: 系统中存在多个名为getInfo的方法，分别在哪些控制器中定义？它们各自对应的API路径是什么？前端调用GET /getInfo时会到达哪个方法？

question: The frontend user.js file sends a DELETE request to /system/user/{userId}. Can you trace this to the backend? Also check: does the same path /system/user/ with a GET method go to the same controller method or a different one?

question: The frontend job module calls a function to get job details by ID using a dynamically constructed URL. Can you trace this specific frontend request through the REQUESTS_API relation in the static index to its backend handler?

expected_uncertainties.description: The frontend getJob function constructs the URL dynamically: '/monitor/job/' + jobId
The static index marks this as dynamic_url and does NOT store a REQUESTS_API relation for it
While the backend endpoint GET /monitor/job/{jobId} exists in SysJobController, the trace cannot be verified through the index

annotation.notes: Unanswerable trace: dynamic URL prevents index-based verification. Frontend request included.

question: To fix the security vulnerability where scheduled jobs can execute arbitrary Java methods via the invoke target, what is the minimal set of code changes needed while still preserving the scheduling functionality?

Service execution mechanism could be sandboxed

annotation.notes: Unanswerable: 'minimal change' is subjective and the execution mechanism is in service layer not visible from controller alone.

question: When a role's permissions are modified, automatically refresh the permissions of all currently online users who hold that role, so that permission changes take effect immediately without requiring users to log out and back in.

gold_files.reason: Observed changed file in historical commit HC-001
Observed changed file in historical commit HC-001

annotation.notes: Focused backend logic change in the authentication/authorization layer. The fix moves permission-refresh logic from the controller into TokenService where it belongs, and adds iteration over online users holding the changed role. Clear security-relevant intent. Good example of a small but high-impact change that requires understanding of the token/session model.

change_plan_provenance.change_request: When a role's permissions are modified, automatically refresh the permissions of all currently online users who hold that role, so that permission changes take effect immediately without requiring users to log out and back in.
change_plan_provenance.source_commit_message: 角色权限变更后刷新所有持有该角色的在线用户权限
